summary: Unused dayjs-lookalike dependency pulled in at install time
details: @mastra/agent-browser@0.3.2 declares a dependency on 'easy-day-js@^1.11.21' in package.json, but the package's own compiled code in dist/index.js never imports or references easy-day-js (only @mastra/core, agent-browser, and zod are used). The dependency name resembles the popular 'dayjs' library and pulling it in at install time means every installer of @mastra/agent-browser also fetches and executes whatever easy-day-js contains. The wrapper package's own code shows no install hooks, no obfuscation, and no exfiltration behavior, so the installer-side risk depends entirely on the contents of easy-day-js. Routing to human review so the transitive can be evaluated and, if malicious, both packages handled together.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
